title: Searching & Replacing 🔍
description: Find, replace, and count within strings.

slides.title: Finding Text 🔍
slides.content:
# Search Methods

| Method | Purpose |
|--------|--------|
| `find(x)` | Returns index of first occurrence, or -1 |
| `index(x)` | Like find, but raises error if not found |
| `count(x)` | Counts how many times x appears |
| `replace(old, new)` | Replaces all occurrences |

```
text = "Hello World, Hello Python"
text.find("Hello")       # 0 (first position)
text.find("Java")        # -1 (not found)
text.count("Hello")      # 2
text.replace("Hello", "Hi") # "Hi World, Hi Python"
```

slides.title: Search & Replace 🎬
slides.content: Find and replace text within strings:
slides.codeSnippet:
sentence = "The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog"

print("Find 'fox':", sentence.find("fox"))
print("Count 'the':", sentence.lower().count("the"))

# Replace
new = sentence.replace("fox", "cat")
print("Replaced:", new)

# Chain methods!
result = sentence.upper().replace("THE", "A")
print("Chained:", result)

quizData.question: What does find() return if the substring is NOT found?
quizData.explanation: find() returns -1 when the substring isn't found. Use index() if you want an error instead.

quizData.question: What does "aabaa".count("a") return?
quizData.explanation: count() finds all non-overlapping occurrences. 'aabaa' has 4 a's.
